title: Tapping for the Home Comparison Spiral
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the feeling that your home doesn't measure up
excerpt: An EFT tapping sequence for the moment when someone else's home triggers the comparison spiral: the home tour, the Instagram kitchen, the friend's renovation. Taps through the inadequacy, the wanting, and toward being able to see your own home for what it gives you.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use after a moment of home comparison — a house tour, a renovation reveal, a visit that left you seeing your own home as lacking.

content.text: An EFT tapping sequence for the spiral that starts when someone else's home makes yours feel insufficient. The comparison is rarely about the house itself. It tends to be about worth, and whether what you have built is enough evidence that you are doing life correctly. This sequence taps on the comparison, not the home.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though her home makes mine feel like it isn't enough, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the comparison spiral started and I couldn't stop it, I accept that this is where I am right now.
Even though I want more for my home and feel ashamed that I want it, I am willing to look at this clearly.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: Her home is so much more than mine.
Side of Eye: Mine looks shabby by comparison.
Under Eye: I can't stop noticing all the ways mine falls short.
Under Nose: The kitchen, the garden, the things I don't have.
Chin: It's making me feel behind, like I've failed at something.
Collarbone: The wanting I feel but can't quite justify.
Under Arm: The way my home stopped feeling enough the moment I walked into hers.
Top of Head: The spiral I didn't ask for.
Reframe
Eyebrow: Her home is a fact about her life, not a verdict on mine.
Side of Eye: My home does not become less by comparison.
Under Eye: The comparison is telling me about my own unmet wants. That is useful.
Under Nose: Wanting more is not the same as what I have being wrong.
Chin: My home gives me things I often don't notice when I'm not comparing.
Collarbone: The wanting is information, not a measure of how I'm doing.
Under Arm: My home is the one I actually live in, and it holds real things.
Top of Head: I can want more without the comparison running me.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
